behavior_summary: U8P_BRIBOOK5::func0080 spawns a Book::read process to present a long block of lore text to the player, passing a string pointer containing the book text and the book object (this).
The returned process id is stored in local 'temp'.
Immediately after spawning Book::read the function calls suspend, yielding/pausing the current process. This function appears to be the 'read/activate' handler for the book object.
notable_control_flow: Spawn external process Book::read(...) and capture its pid in local 'temp'.
Call suspend right after spawning, causing the current process to yield (likely to let the reader UI/process run).
No branching, menu interactions, or global flag reads/writes occur in this function.
open_questions: Exact trigger: is U8P_BRIBOOK5::func0080 invoked by a player 'use/activate/read' action (inferred but not explicitly encoded)?
Does the calling/suspended process later resume or inspect 'temp' (the stored pid), or is 'temp' only used for local bookkeeping here?
How Book::read schedules its own completion or returns control — does the original process resume automatically or remain suspended until an external signal?

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK5::func0080
main_function.purpose: Start a Book::read process to display the book's text to the player and then yield/suspend the current process.
entry_conditions: Inferred: invoked when the player activates/uses/reads this book object.
Spawns Book::read with (string pointer -> long lore text, this -> the book object).
Suspends the current process immediately after spawning.

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK5::func0080
function_map.role: activate/read handler that spawns a Book::read reader process and yields

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: UI/process that displays book text to the player and associates the reader with the source book object

external_calls.purpose: Display the provided long book text to the player and start a reader process tied to the book object (string pointer, this).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BRIBOOK5::func0080 spawns a Book::read process to present a long block of lore text to the player, passing a string pointer containing the book text and the book object (this).
